name: Precision Agriculture
what: Data-driven crop management and yield optimisation.
how: Sensors, drones and AI guide inputs field-by-field.

name: Alternative Proteins
how: Cell culture and fermentation replace some livestock.

name: Food Supply Automation
how: Robotics and tracking cut waste and labour.

name: C4 Photosynthesis Engineering
what: Re-engineering staple crops such as rice to use the more efficient C4 photosynthesis found in maize and sugarcane.
how: Researchers add and tune the genes and leaf anatomy of the C4 pathway into a C3 crop to raise water- and nitrogen-use efficiency.

name: Nitrogen-Fixing Cereals
what: Giving cereal crops the ability to obtain nitrogen from the air, reducing dependence on synthetic fertiliser.
how: Approaches engineer cereal roots to host nitrogen-fixing microbes or express fixation pathways, mimicking what legumes do naturally.

name: Gene-Edited Crops
what: Crops improved with precise gene edits — for flavour, shelf life or disease resistance — without adding foreign DNA.
how: CRISPR edits switch off or tweak the plant's own genes; edited lines are field-trialled and advanced toward market.

name: Cultivated Meat
what: Real animal meat grown from cells in bioreactors instead of raising and slaughtering animals.
how: Animal cells are grown in nutrient media on scaffolds inside bioreactors and harvested into meat products; early facilities run at pilot scale.
use: Alternative protein with reduced land use

name: Precision-Fermentation Dairy Proteins
what: Making real milk proteins with engineered microbes so dairy products can be produced without cows.
how: Microbes are engineered to secrete whey or casein proteins, then grown in fermenters and purified for use in animal-free dairy foods.
